reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generates the correct text content, ensuring Content Accuracy. However, regarding the specific paralinguistic instruction to emphasize the word 'absolutely', the performance is poor. In the segment from 00:00 to 00:01 ('You absolutely'), the word is delivered with a flat pitch and uniform speed that blends indistinguishably with the rest of the sentence. There is no noticeable increase in volume, duration, or pitch change that would constitute 'emphasis'. Consequently, the specific requirement of the prompt is not met effectively. The voice also has a slightly mechanical quality.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the text accurately and with high naturalness. Crucially, it adheres strictly to the paralinguistic instruction. Between 00:00 and 00:02, the speaker places a very distinct emphasis on the word 'absolutely'. The model achieves this by elongating the word, clearly articulating each syllable ('ab-so-lute-ly'), and employing a downward pitch inflection that conveys seriousness and weight. This contrast with the surrounding text makes the emphasis unmistakable and vivid, perfectly satisfying the core requirement of the prompt.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. The user's prompt specifically requested emphasis on the word 'absolutely'. Model 1 failed to provide this specific paralinguistic feature; the word was read with the same flat intonation as the rest of the sentence, making the execution of the command ineffective. In contrast, Model 2 demonstrated excellent controllability by significantly altering the prosody (elongation and clear enunciation) of the target word to create a strong, natural emphasis. Model 2 also exhibited superior overall naturalness compared to the slightly robotic tone of Model 1.
